name: Cabrera de Mar to Castell de Burriac
name_original: De Cabrera de Mar al castillo de Burriac
description:
This circular route starts and ends in Cabrera de Mar and allows you to discover two very interesting spots: on the one hand, the ruins of Burriac Castle and, on the other, the impressive Creu de Montcabrer viewpoint.

To reach the castle, you walk through a Mediterranean forest to the top of the hill where it stands. Its 401 meters of altitude provide incredible views of the area.

Then, retrace your steps to descend the hill and continue to the next point on the route. The Creu de Montcabrer viewpoint rises 311 meters and the views are breathtaking. On one side, you can see the Mediterranean Sea. On the other, you can see the castle you left behind a while ago. Furthermore, the iron cross that rises above the viewpoint has a long history of legends. It's a magical place.
region_intro: Hiking around Cabrera De Mar offers diverse terrain, situated between the Mediterranean Sea and the Litoral Mountain Range. The region features uneven landscapes with pine and holm oak forests, providing a variety of natural environments for outdoor activities. Hikers can explore coastal paths, historical sites, and the foothills of the Serra de Sant Mateu. This geographical position allows for routes ranging from gentle coastal walks to more challenging mountain trails. Best hiking trails around Cabrera De Mar The most popular hiking route is Cabrera de Mar to Castell de Burriac, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 44 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to the historic Castell de Burriac, offering panoramic views of the Montseny mountain range. Another top favourite among local hikers is Burriac Pass – Burriac Castle loop from Cabrera de Mar, a moderate 4.6 miles (7.4 km) path. This loop explores the area around Burriac Castle, traversing varied forest terrain. Local hikers also love the Burriac Castle – Old Path to Castell de Burriac loop from Cabrera de Mar, a 1.6 miles (2.6 km) trail leading through forested slopes, often completed in about 50 minutes. Hiking around Cabrera De Mar is defined by its coastal paths, the Litoral Mountain Range, and dense pine and holm oak woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Cabrera De Mar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Cabrera De Mar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Cabrera De Mar?
A: There are over 30 hiking trails around Cabrera De Mar, offering a diverse range of experiences from coastal walks to mountain ascents. You'll find options for various fitness levels, including 20 easy routes and 12 moderate trails.

Q: What historical landmarks or points of interest can I explore while hiking in Cabrera De Mar?
A: Many trails in Cabrera De Mar lead to significant historical sites. A prominent destination is the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1126577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burriac Castle&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views and nearly a thousand years of history. You can also discover archaeological sites like the Roman villa of Can Benet and Iberian thermal baths at Clos de Ca l'Arenau, or the peaceful Ermita de Santa Elena.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Cabrera De Mar?
A: Yes, Cabrera De Mar offers many easy routes suitable for families. For a shorter, accessible option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14955465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burriac Castle – Old Path to Castell de Burriac loop from Cabrera de Mar&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 1.6-mile trail through forested slopes, often completed in about 50 minutes.

Q: Where can I find more challenging hikes around Cabrera De Mar?
A: For those seeking a more strenuous adventure, the region offers at least one difficult trail. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1477131" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Creu de Montcabrer Summit – Burriac Castle loop from Vilassar de Mar&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 6.6-mile route with significant elevation gain, providing a rewarding experience for experienced hikers.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Cabrera De Mar?
A: Yes, several circular routes allow you to start and end in the same location. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6957258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burriac Pass – Burriac Castle loop from Cabrera de Mar&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 4.6-mile path that explores the area around Burriac Castle through varied forest terrain.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes will I encounter on hikes in Cabrera De Mar?
A: Cabrera De Mar's unique geography means you'll experience diverse landscapes. Hikes can take you through the pine and holm oak forests of the Litoral Mountain Range, offering uneven terrain and mountain views. You can also find picturesque coastal paths connecting to neighboring towns, providing a mix of sandy and rocky beach scenery.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Cabrera De Mar?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the historical significance of sites like Burriac Castle, and the beautiful blend of mountain and coastal scenery.

Q: Are there any coastal walks or beach trails in Cabrera De Mar?
A: Yes, Cabrera De Mar provides access to lovely coastal paths. You can enjoy walks along beaches like Platja de Cabrera, Platja de Santa Margarida, El Molí, and Dels Vinyals. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19203004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Astillero Beach – Club Nàutic Vilassar de Mar loop from Cabrera de Mar - Vilassar de Mar&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3-mile coastal hike.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Cabrera De Mar?
A: Many trails in the Litoral Mountain Range and surrounding natural areas are generally dog-friendly, allowing you to explore with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and check local regulations for specific areas or protected zones.

Q: What are the typical durations for hikes in Cabrera De Mar?
A: Hiking durations vary significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. Shorter, easy loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14955465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burriac Castle – Old Path to Castell de Burriac loop&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in under an hour. Moderate trails, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1734226244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabrera de Mar to Castell de Burriac&lt;/a&gt;, typically take around 2 hours 45 minutes, while longer, difficult routes can extend to over 3 hours.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or panoramic spots along the hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. The hike to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1126577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burriac Castle&lt;/a&gt; is renowned for its breathtaking panoramic views of the Montseny mountain range and the Maresme landscapes. Many trails within the Litoral Mountain Range also offer elevated perspectives of the coastline and surrounding valleys.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cabrera De Mar?
A: Cabrera De Mar is pleasant for hiking for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures, making them ideal for longer excursions. Even in summer, the coastal breezes and shaded forest paths provide comfortable conditions, though it's always wise to start early to avoid the midday heat.
tips: To get to Burriac Castle, you have to go through a typically Mediterranean forest made up of pines, oaks and bushes. Follow the trail signs and in no time you will see the castle rising above the hill.

The remains of Burriac Castle are almost a thousand years old. They are located at the top of Burriac Hill. One of the best ways to get to the castle is by following the local trail SL-C 115 from Cabrera de Mar.

La Creu de Montcabrer (311 meters) is a viewpoint with spectacular views of the sea and mountains. The iron cross that presides over it has a long history of legends related to the towns of Cabrera de
Sea and Cabrils.

waypoints.name: Trail with views of Burriac Castle
